questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 50° and angle B measures 65°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So angle C = 180° − 50° − 65° = 65°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one acute angle measuring 35°. What is the measure of the other acute angle?
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the two acute angles sum to 90°. So the other acute angle = 90° − 35° = 55°.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle, the vertex angle (the angle between the two equal sides) measures 40°. What is the measure of each base angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the base angles are equal. If the vertex angle is 40°, then each base angle = (180° − 40°) ÷ 2 = 70°.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One of the alternate interior angles measures 78°. What is the measure of the other alternate interior angle?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal when formed by parallel lines and a transversal. Both angles measure 78°.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, the exterior angle at vertex Q measures 120°. What is the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles at P and R?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. Therefore, angle P + angle R = 120°.

questions.question: An equilateral triangle has sides of length 8 cm. What is the measure of each interior angle?
questions.explanation: All interior angles of an equilateral triangle are equal. Since they sum to 180°, each angle = 180° ÷ 3 = 60°.
